reforms.description: The International Campus of Excellence Programme of 2009 seeks to promote cooperation between public and private institutions, introducing joint public-private financing of training projects to enhance human capital in research and development.
reforms.component_name: Joint Public-Private Financing of Training Projects
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing human capital through training projects is expected to improve productivity and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been actively promoting public-private cooperation since its launch.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly enhances cooperation and funding mechanisms for training but does not represent a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The International Campus of Excellence Programme of 2009 seeks to promote co-operation of public and private institutions.

reforms.description: The Spanish government introduced employer social security contribution rebates to promote hiring in strategic sectors, including research and development and renewable energies, thereby supporting innovation and job creation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing hiring in strategic sectors, the reform is expected to enhance innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The rebates have been actively applied to encourage hiring in targeted sectors.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform provides significant incentives for hiring but does not fundamentally change the innovation support system.
reforms.source_quote: Employers’ SSC rebates to promote new jobs in strategic sectors and activities (such as research and development and renewable energies).
